Scenario.title: Night Dragon Targeting Transportation Systems
Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Simple A basic-skilled threat group named Night Dragon initiates a targeted attack against transportation systems through phishing, followed by execution of malicious payloads and denial-of-service attacks to disrupt operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Process, Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Reg.exe Utility, Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker sends deceptive emails impersonating legitimate transport vendors to trick employees into revealing credentials, exploiting human error to establish initial access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-635: Alternative Execution Due to Deceptive Filenames
stage.description: The attacker executes a malicious file disguised as a legitimate process using the Reg.exe utility, leveraging compromised credentials to manipulate scheduling/monitoring systems.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker overwhelms network resources with excessive traffic, disrupting traffic control systems and causing operational downtime.

Scenario.summary: A basic-skilled threat group exploits phishing to gain initial access, executes malicious payloads to manipulate systems, and triggers denial-of-service attacks to disrupt transportation operations.
